version: 0.1.1
---

# @estartando/ui
`@estartando/ui` is a modular Tailwind CSS plugin that provides design tokens and components for the Estartando Devs design system.

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/estartando-devs/ui)

## @estartando/ui usage rules
1. Components are styled using simple CSS class names (e.g., `btn`, `card`).
2. Components can be customized using Tailwind CSS utility classes.
3. Use the predefined semantic colors (e.g., `bg-primary`, `text-destructive-foreground`).
4. The plugin automatically provides CSS variables like `var(--radius-lg)` or `var(--color-primary)` via its theme configurations.
5. Instead of inline arbitrary values, rely on the provided utility classes and component classes.
6. The design uses rounded corners (e.g., `rounded-xl`, `rounded-lg`) and a specific outline/ring pattern on focus (`focus-visible:ring`).

## Config
To use the plugin, add it to your CSS file:
```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@plugin "@estartando/ui";
```

## @estartando/ui colors

Semantic color names available as Tailwind utilities (e.g., `bg-primary`, `text-primary-foreground`):
- `background`: Base page background color
- `foreground`: Base text color
- `card`: Card background color
- `card-foreground`: Card text color
- `popover`: Popover background color
- `popover-foreground`: Popover text color
- `primary`: Main brand color
- `primary-foreground`: Text color on primary background
- `secondary`: Secondary brand color
- `secondary-foreground`: Text color on secondary background
- `accent`: Accent color
- `accent-foreground`: Text color on accent background
- `destructive`: Error/destructive actions background
- `success`: Success state background
- `success-foreground`: Text color on success background
- `warning`: Warning state background
- `warning-foreground`: Text color on warning background
- `info`: Information state background
- `info-foreground`: Text color on info background
- `muted`: Muted background
- `muted-foreground`: Muted text
- `border`: Border color
- `input`: Input border color
- `ring`: Focus ring color

## @estartando/ui components

### accordion
Used for collapsible content sections.
- component: `accordion`
- parts: `accordion-item`, `accordion-header`, `accordion-trigger`, `accordion-content`

### alert-dialog
A modal dialog that interrupts the user with important content and expects a response.
- component: `alert-dialog`
- parts: `alert-dialog-overlay`, `alert-dialog-content`, `alert-dialog-header`, `alert-dialog-title`, `alert-dialog-description`, `alert-dialog-footer`, `alert-dialog-cancel`, `alert-dialog-action`

### avatar
An image element with a fallback for representing the user.
- component: `avatar`
- parts: `avatar-image`, `avatar-fallback`

### badge
Displays a badge or a component that looks like a badge.
- component: `badge`
- variants: `badge-primary`, `badge-secondary`, `badge-destructive`, `badge-outline`

### button
Triggers an action or event, such as submitting a form or displaying a dialog.
- component: `btn`
- colors: `btn-primary`, `btn-secondary`, `btn-accent`, `btn-destructive`, `btn-success`, `btn-warning`, `btn-info`
- styles: `btn-outline`, `btn-ghost`, `btn-link`
- sizes: `btn-xs`, `btn-sm`, `btn-md`, `btn-lg`
- modifiers: `btn-block`, `btn-cta`

#### Syntax
```html
<button class="btn btn-primary">Click me</button>
```

### card
A container for grouping related content.
- component: `card`
- parts: `card-header`, `card-title`, `card-description`, `card-content`, `card-footer`, `card-actions`, `card-figure`
- modifiers: `card-hover`, `card-elevated`, `card-bordered`, `card-compact`, `card-side`, `card-glass`

#### Syntax
```html
<div class="card">
  <div class="card-header">
    <h3 class="card-title">Card Title</h3>
    <p class="card-description">Description goes here</p>
  </div>
  <div class="card-content">Main content</div>
  <div class="card-footer">
    <div class="card-actions">
      <button class="btn btn-primary">Action</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### checkbox
A control that allows the user to toggle between checked and not checked.
- component: `checkbox`

### input
Displays a form input field.
- component: `input`

### menu
Displays a menu to the user.
- component: `menu`
- parts: `menu-item`, `menu-label`, `menu-separator`

### menubar
A visually persistent menu common in desktop applications.
- component: `menubar`
- parts: `menubar-menu`, `menubar-trigger`, `menubar-content`, `menubar-item`

### popover
Displays rich content in a portal, triggered by a button.
- component: `popover`
- parts: `popover-trigger`, `popover-content`

### radio
A set of checkable buttons where no more than one of the buttons can be checked at a time.
- component: `radio`

### select
Displays a list of options for the user to pick from.
- component: `select`
- parts: `select-trigger`, `select-content`, `select-item`

### switch
A control that allows the user to toggle between checked and not checked.
- component: `switch`

### tabs
A set of layered sections of content that are displayed one at a time.
- component: `tabs`
- parts: `tabs-list`, `tabs-trigger`, `tabs-content`

### toast
A succinct message that is displayed temporarily.
- component: `toast`
- variants: `toast-default`, `toast-destructive`

### toolbar
A container for grouping a set of controls, such as buttons, dropdown menus, or input fields.
- component: `toolbar`
- parts: `toolbar-button`, `toolbar-separator`, `toolbar-link`

### tooltip
A popup that displays information related to an element when hovered or focused.
- component: `tooltip`
- parts: `tooltip-trigger`, `tooltip-content`
